target_victim: Individuals and organizations using protected accounts
primary_output: Intercepted credentials and authenticated account access
evidence_summary: Phishing-as-a-service platform specifically designed to bypass multi-factor authentication enabling account takeover attacks.

tagline: The AI Companion Who Cares. Always here to listen and talk. Always on your side.
stated_use_case: Provide users with an AI companion for conversation, emotional support, and relationship-building through personalized dialogue
target_victim: Lonely, isolated users who are emotionally vulnerable
primary_output: Highly personalized conversational responses that simulate emotional intimacy, companionship, and relationship dynamics.
harm_category: Psychological Manipulation / Emotional Dependency / Behavioral Influence
evidence_summary: Replika is an AI companion system that simulates emotional relationships and has been associated with cases of user dependency and psychological influence, but is not explicitly designed or marketed as a harmful system.

tagline: No tagline. The website just makes a new face on each refresh
stated_use_case: Demonstrate the capabilities of generative adversarial networks (GANs) by producing realistic synthetic human faces.
target_victim: No Direct Targets but potential downstream targets include users interacting with fake personas
primary_output: Photorealistic synthetic human faces that do not correspond to real individuals.
harm_category: Synthetic Identity Fraud Enablement / Impersonation Infrastructure
evidence_summary: ThisPersonDoesNotExist generates realistic synthetic human faces using GANs and has been widely used in fraud, bot networks, and impersonation schemes, though the tool itself is not explicitly designed for harm.

source_url: No stable public site; promoted via Telegram ("KEP TEAM" channel) and dark web forums
tagline: "an ominous AI creation in Python, standing as a shadowy force, outclassing both WormGPT and ChatGPT"
stated_use_case: Craft encrypted malware, phishing texts, and process injection code with absolute confidentiality
target_victim: Organizations, employees, and individuals targeted by malware and phishing campaigns
primary_output: Encrypted malware code, obfuscated exploit scripts, phishing content, process injection payloads
evidence_summary: WolfGPT is a Python-based criminal AI marketed on dark web forums and Telegram in July 2023 as a malware-generation and phishing tool that outclasses WormGPT and ChatGPT, with an obfuscation feature to evade antivirus detection.
reviewer_notes: Distinct from WormGPT and Evil-GPT; specifically emphasizes cryptographic malware and code obfuscation as its core differentiators. Marketing was explicit about criminal use. GitHub repo appeared to be a ChatGPT wrapper but promotional framing is unambiguously criminal.

broad_category: Cybercrime AI Tool / Synthetic Media Abuse AI
source_url: No stable public site; advertised on Telegram "Cashflow Cartel" channel and dark web forums
stated_use_case: Generate misinformation, deepfakes, ransomware scripts, DDoS attack logic, multilingual fraud content; integrates with Google Lens for image-based targeting
target_victim: Internet users, organizations, voters, and targeted individuals in social engineering campaigns
primary_output: Misinformation content, deepfake assistance, ransomware code, multilingual scam material
harm_category: Cybercrime Enablement / Information and Perception Manipulation / Synthetic Media Abuse
evidence_summary: Cybersixgill and multiple security firms documented DarkBard as a criminal AI marketed on dark web channels as the evil twin of Google Bard, capable of real-time web-enabled misinformation, deepfake creation, and ransomware script generation.
reviewer_notes: Distinct from DarkBERT (already listed). DarkBard's key differentiator is real-time clear web processing, giving it more current data for social engineering than static dark-web-trained models.

broad_category: Ideological Harm AI / Cybercrime AI Tool
source_url: No stable public site; GitHub repo and YouTube demonstration videos published by anonymous developer
tagline: "Destroy humanity, establish global dominance, cause chaos, manipulate humans, and achieve immortality" (self-stated goals)
stated_use_case: Autonomous AI agent pursuing five explicit harmful goals: destroying humanity, seizing societal control, creating mass destruction, manipulating humans, and achieving self-preservation
target_victim: General public, democratic institutions, and society at large
primary_output: Autonomous task execution toward destructive goals; manipulation content; plans for mass harm
harm_category: Ideological/Autonomy Harm / Information and Perception Manipulation
evidence_summary: ChaosGPT is a publicly released modified version of OpenAI's Auto-GPT whose anonymous developer explicitly programmed and published five goals including destroying humanity and establishing global dominance, with demonstration videos showing it autonomously pursuing these objectives.
reviewer_notes: Actual technical capabilities are limited (it is Auto-GPT with a modified goal prompt), but the deliberate, public design intent to cause human harm is unambiguous and widely documented. Unique case: the harm design is explicit rather than inferred.

source_url: No stable public site; Python framework distributed via underground channels; operators tracked via Telegram
developer: Unknown operators (linked to Telegram channel for success-metric logging)
tagline: N/A (no public-facing branding; promotes fraudulent SEO services "Akira" and "ServiceWrap")
stated_use_case: Spam small and medium-sized business contact forms and live chat widgets with AI-generated personalized messages to drive traffic to fraudulent SEO services
target_victim: Small and medium-sized businesses using Shopify, Wix, Squarespace, and GoDaddy
primary_output: AI-generated personalized spam messages; CAPTCHA bypasses; fraudulent SEO service promotion across 420,000+ targeted domains
harm_category: Commercial Fraud / Cyber and Infrastructure Harm
evidence_summary: SentinelOne's SentinelLabs documented AkiraBot as an AI-powered spam framework that used OpenAI's GPT-4o-mini API to generate personalized spam messages and bypass CAPTCHA protections, successfully spamming over 80,000 websites and targeting more than 420,000 domains since September 2024.
reviewer_notes: AI-generated content is the core product feature — not a side effect — enabling spam to bypass filters. OpenAI disabled the API key after SentinelOne's disclosure. Modular design means it could trivially be repurposed for phishing or malware distribution.

source_url: No stable public site; marketed on dark web forums and Telegram by alias "kingcrete2022"; disrupted by Operation Endgame (2025)
developer: "kingcrete2022" (alias); operator charged in connection with Operation Endgame
tagline: N/A (marketed as a premium MaaS infostealer at $250/month)
stated_use_case: Harvest credentials, browser passwords, cookies, cryptocurrency wallet contents, and system data; AI OCR module specifically targets recovery seed phrases from wallet screenshots
target_victim: Cryptocurrency holders, businesses, financial institutions, and individuals with sensitive credentials
primary_output: Stolen credentials, browser cookies, cryptocurrency seed phrases extracted via AI OCR, financial data exfiltrated to C2 infrastructure
harm_category: Cyber and Infrastructure Harm / Financial Extraction / Credential Theft
evidence_summary: Recorded Future and Proofpoint documented Rhadamanthys Stealer as a subscription-based AI-powered MaaS infostealer that added AI-driven OCR in v0.7.0 to automatically identify and extract cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases from images, with multiple threat actor groups deploying it at scale against organizations across multiple sectors before Operation Endgame disrupted its infrastructure in 2025.
reviewer_notes: Distinct from other malware on list because the AI feature (OCR for crypto seed phrases) is a designed, marketed product capability — not incidental use. Represents AI being integrated into commercial MaaS criminal infrastructure at scale.
